comment: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.
Text is still visible and readable when resized to at least 200%.
Attachments are accessible using a keyboard only.
Focusable form fields receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.
The purpose of each attachment can be determined from the text alone.
Focus is visible when navigating using a keyboard.
When an attachment receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context.
Detailed labels or instructions are provided when a field requires user input.
For all attachment elements the name and role can be programmatically determined.
Text is visible/readable when the line spacing is at least 1.5 times the font size, letter spacing is at least 0.12 times
the font size, and word spacing is at least 0.16 times the font size.
For fields that contain labels the name contains the text that is presented visually.
